drivers.label: Revenue growth (Yr 1–10)
drivers.comment: GOOGL grew ~15% in FY25. Each scenario fades from a Yr-1 rate to mature growth by Yr 10. Base ≈ trend; Bull assumes AI/Cloud re-accelerates; Bear is conservative.

drivers.label: EBIT margin (Yr 1–10)
drivers.comment: All scenarios assume steady operating-margin expansion despite heavy AI capex. The Bull path ending near 39% would be an all-time high — plausible only if AI drives real operating leverage.

drivers.label: Capex (Yr 1–10, % rev)
drivers.comment: Elevated near-term for the AI data-centre build-out, then declining. That decline path is the single biggest swing factor in the whole model.

drivers.label: D&A (% of revenue)
drivers.comment: Small, stable — ~5% of revenue. Not a swing factor.

drivers.label: Net working capital (% of revenue)
drivers.comment: GOOGL actually runs near-zero / negative working capital (deferred revenue funds it). Modelling a 15–18% drag understates value — the model is conservative here.

drivers.label: Tax rate
drivers.comment: 21% US statutory; GOOGL's effective rate has run ~16–19%, so this trims NOPAT a touch (biases value down).

drivers.label: Risk-free rate
drivers.comment: ≈ the 10-year Treasury yield. ~4% is consistent with current levels.

drivers.label: Equity risk premium
drivers.comment: 3.9–4.3% ≈ Damodaran's implied ERP of 4.23% (2026). This is where current market conditions legitimately enter the model.

drivers.label: Beta
drivers.comment: ~1.0–1.1; GOOGL roughly tracks the market. Slightly lower in the Bull case (less perceived risk).

drivers.label: WACC (derived)
drivers.comment: CAPM build-up nets to ~7.8–8.9% across scenarios. Lower WACC in Bull compounds with higher growth to lift value.

drivers.label: Terminal growth
drivers.verdict: Base OK · Bull at the ceiling
drivers.comment: Terminal g should not exceed the ~4% risk-free / long-run nominal GDP. Bull's 3.5% sits right at that edge.

drivers.label: Scenario probability
drivers.comment: Weights already lean bullish (Bull 40% / Base 45%). The market's high-20s× multiple implies even more weight on the Bull path — that's the AI fever, priced.

comps.note: The chosen multiple is where 'market hotness' legitimately belongs — not a fudge factor on the DCF. At 25× (a quality-tech multiple) comps land almost exactly on the mid-year DCF, so both methods triangulate near the same value. The market currently pays a high-20s× forward multiple; that premium over the central estimate IS the AI-regime optimism, made explicit and auditable rather than hidden in a sentiment multiplier.
